research_goal: Explore whether this synthetic persona has any meaningful pain in day-to-day personal finance, starting from real recent life events and surfacing the participant's own stories, pain points, root causes, coping methods, and why those pains matter or do not matter.
product_context: No predefined solution is assumed. This is an exploratory interview about the participant's current day-to-day personal finance life across account opening, spending, saving, transfers, bill payment, investing, record-keeping, household coordination, trust, and other related routines.
synthetic_only_disclaimer: Synthetic-user interview for AI pre-validation only; not human market evidence.

key_insights: This synthetic run surfaced real bookkeeping friction around confirmation, record-keeping, and later reconciliation.
Manual screenshots and handwritten logs appear to function as trust-preserving workarounds rather than pure habit.
evidence_gaps: trust_boundary remained under-specified in this synthetic run.
first_value_requirement remained under-specified in this synthetic run.
pricing_signal remained under-specified in this synthetic run.
retention_risk remained under-specified in this synthetic run.
insight_report.next_experiment: Run a tighter follow-up interview on trust boundary, setup burden, and repeat-use conditions.
insight_report.synthetic_only_disclaimer: Synthetic pre-validation only; not human market evidence.
potential_over_optimism_risks: Synthetic users may have understood the concept too quickly; no meaningful clarification or misunderstanding behaviour appeared.
Wording misunderstanding and first-use confusion were not meaningfully simulated in this run.
All payment and adoption signals here are still stated intention rather than observed behavior.
No real prototype behavior was tested, so setup confusion, wording breakdown, and actual drop-off remain unobserved.

findings.description: Synthesis proposes specific design implications (e.g., 'product should allow pending state', 'separate outward transparency from internal verification') before the concept has been introduced or tested. While logically derived from behavior, these cross into speculative product design and should be explicitly labeled as post-concept hypotheses rather than current insights.

Coverage status correctly flags missing `trust_boundary`, `repeat_use_condition`, and `service_embedding`. The facilitator trace does not log a concrete plan for probing these post-concept-introduction, creating a minor continuity gap for the next run.

next_interview_focus: Introduce the concept neutrally only after `concept_intro_allowed` flips to true. Immediately probe trust boundaries (data sharing limits, family/partner visibility), repeat-use triggers (specific friction points that would cause abandonment vs continued reliance), and service embedding (how it slots into the existing MTR/app/notes batch workflow).
coverage_gap_actions: Ask directly: 'What information would you absolutely refuse to share with anyone else?' 'Under what exact circumstance would you delete this tool after trying it?' 'How would you expect this to sit alongside your current month-end spreadsheet or notes app?'
prompt_adjustments: Enforce a strict 'no design implication until concept-tested' rule in synthesis. Add a mandatory checkpoint in the facilitator prompt to explicitly defer concept introduction until prerequisites (pressure, defensible_vs_uncertain) are satisfied, preventing premature product framing.
improvement_hints.turn_budget_guidance: Current 7-turn soft/hard limit is optimal for the pre-concept phase. Expand to 10-12 turns only after concept introduction to accommodate trial simulation, retention questioning, pricing calibration, and disconfirmation probes.

quality_evaluation.methodology_notes: Protocol adherence is strong: concept introduction was correctly deferred due to missing prerequisites, and the facilitator maintained behavioral grounding through threshold, contrast, and missing-evidence probes. Synthesis appropriately flags unknowns for pricing/retention and preserves weak problem evidence. Low-severity notes on speculative design implications do not violate core methodology but warrant tighter bounding in future runs.
required_improvements: Review the transcript and synthesis manually before treating this run as calibrated evidence.
quality_evaluation.synthetic_only_disclaimer: Synthetic pre-validation only; not human market evidence.

high_value_missed_followups: Fallback protocol for current workaround failure (e.g., device loss, data corruption, or system outage)
Concrete consequence or near-miss from the deferred reconciliation strategy
Direct driver prioritization when efficiency, cognitive load, and trust/conflict avoidance are all present
likely_misclassified_driver_patterns: Collapsing 'operational rhythm maintenance' as the sole driver without testing its hierarchy against 'trust preservation' or 'cognitive load reduction'
evidence_handling_issues: Accepting self-described coping mechanisms as stable habits without verifying their failure modes or escalation paths
Allowing synthesis outputs to cross into speculative product design before concept introduction and testing
blocked_feedback: Probing why the participant views real-time tracking as a 'chore' rather than a necessary control step (too tied to this specific cognitive load claim)
Assessing the reliability of using a legacy iOS notes app for financial tracking (tool-specific)
Exploring the exact threshold where 'patching later' becomes unacceptable due to external stakeholder pressure (context-specific to tutoring center operations)
